definition: Stable identifier for the clock row.
snapshot_date, analytic_as_of, source_data_report_date, source_publication_date, source_retrieval_date, or generated_at.
YYYY-MM-DD clock value when the clock is date-granular.
Full timestamp when the clock is timestamp-granular.
Manifest source identifier when this clock belongs to a source edition.
How the clock is used, such as headline endpoint, corridor source load, source review, or context.
Interpretation caveat for the clock row.
Stable row identifier within this export.

Reported quantity. Lab-confirmed cases and confirmed deaths are the cumulative epidemiological counts; suspected figures appear only as per-source historical provenance or as point-in-time operational caseload (under investigation, in isolation, active), never summed into confirmed.
Geographic scope represented by the value when available.
Data date, publication date, or snapshot date used for the row.
Single extracted value when the source reports one.
Manifest source identifier, normalized to the source registry.
Other dated sources included in the reconciled range.
Evidence-chain ID, source-manifest reference, or explicit audit-gap marker.
Supported, corrected, needs source, restricted, or derived status.
How the row was produced: extracted, reconciled, or model-derived.
SHA-256 hash for archived public bytes or hash-recorded restricted bytes.
Publisher/source license recorded in the manifest.
Known correction or limitation relevant to the row.

Death-axis basis: confirmed_only for death rows dated on/after 2026-06-02 (laboratory-confirmed death tier), broad_register for earlier death rows (mixed confirmed+suspected register). Empty for non-death rows.
Analytic or display surface that should declare whether it consumed the latest count-bearing source inputs.
updated, updated_snapshot_level, carried_forward, source_attribution_lag, or blocked.
Public JSON summary of latest input counts consumed by this surface.
Public JSON summary of output values produced from those inputs.
Clock/date interpretation used for the surface.
How this surface may be used downstream.
Reason a latest count was not used as an ordinary dated data point, when applicable.
Source review or data-availability blocker preventing full update, when applicable.
Opaque public claim identifier. Detailed audit-registry IDs are intentionally withheld from this export.
Public-health claim or methodology claim being audited.
Public audit status: supported, corrected, needs primary source, or unsupported attribution.
Public source citations or restricted-source placeholders. Detailed source-step IDs are intentionally withheld.
Public source URLs where available; restricted local paths are redacted.
Action a reader should take when interpreting or correcting this claim.
SHA-256 hash recorded by the source manifest.
Whether raw source bytes are redistributed or kept private/restricted.
Publisher-term caveat, especially for restricted or uncertain material.
Manifest-backed source identifier(s), when the source has archived manifest provenance.
Human-readable source chain for watch/context signals.
Whether the value is exact, approximate, bounded, range, or qualitative.
Whether this row can feed a model run, cross-check only, context only, or is blocked.
Downstream use permission for model code.
Other staged/source rows that must be reconciled before model use.
LOVS canonical zone_id (lower_snake_case) matching the corridor_watchlist source_zones list.
INRB-UMIE canonical Nom (post upstream aliases.csv collapse) bridged from lovs_zone_id via data/lovs_zone_alias_bridge.json.
Data date the INRB-UMIE INSP per-zone tables describe (typically earlier than the snapshot publication date).
INSP per-zone cumulative confirmed cases attributable to this LOVS zone at as_of_data_date.
INSP per-zone cumulative confirmed deaths attributable to this LOVS zone at as_of_data_date; spec section 2.3 attribution-lag hierarchy classifies this metric as trailing.
Three-state coverage audit: present_with_data, present_but_zero, or structurally_absent (spec section 2.1).
INRB raw row spellings that the upstream aliases.csv collapsed into this canonical Nom.
LOVS canonical zone_id for a suspected-only surveillance row that is outside the reconciled model.
Vintage of the retired per-zone suspected table used for this display-only row.
Suspected cases on the retired upstream per-zone cumulative-suspected tier. Not reconciled and never summed into national totals.
Confirmed cases for this surveillance row; zero by definition for suspected-only surveillance zones.
How this row may be used. Current value is display_only_surveillance.
Plain-language caveat explaining the retired tier and never-summed contract.
Data date of the INSP per-zone reconciliation.
INSP national rollup value at this metric and date.
Sum of zone-attributed values across LOVS-bridged zones at this date.
national_at_data_date minus sum_per_zone; the reconciliation contract (spec section 5.1) requires this to be non-negative for every metric.
Spec section 2.3 attribution-lag hierarchy: timely, near_timely, or trailing.
Proportion of national rollup that is zone-attributed; complementary to unallocated_residual / national.
Cross-metric narrative; required to mention the 1-3 week INRB clinical review queue lag for confirmed_deaths.
Data date reported by the SitRep.
Publication timestamp/date recorded for the SitRep.
Reviewed narrative or operational-note section name.
One-based item index within the section.
Sanitized public narrative text or operational note.
Evidence-chain ID backing the reviewed SitRep promotion.
Public source URL for the SitRep PDF/post when available.
Public-use boundary for the row, including redaction/licensing caveats.
